title: A Polish Electroencephalography, Alzheimer’s Risk-genes, Lifestyle and Neuroimaging (PEARL-Neuro) Database
description: The PEARL-Neuro Database comprises neuroimaging and genetic data from 192 healthy middle-aged individuals (50-63 years) enriched for dementia risk factors. The dataset includes apolipoprotein E and PICALM genotypes, psychometric assessments, and a neuroimaging subgroup (N=79) with resting-state and task-based EEG and fMRI data acquired during cognitive tasks and rest conditions, along with clinical blood biomarkers.
methods_description: Data collection included genetic analysis (APOE and PICALM polymorphisms), psychometric testing (memory, intelligence, mood, personality, stress coping), and neuroimaging protocols. The neuroimaging subgroup underwent EEG and fMRI acquisition during resting-state conditions (eyes open and eyes closed) and cognitive tasks (multi-source interference task and Sternberg's memory task). Blood samples were analyzed for hematological parameters, lipid profiles, and HSV serology.
